lines: A five-pointed star is daubed point-down on the wall, ringed and dripping, the red still tacky in the cold. A few thin threads of black-gold have darkened the lines where the paint bit into the stone, the way a fresh Host wound beads a little gold before it closes.

It is painted over an older mark, a litany-cross scratched deep into the plaster by the people who were kept here. The cult walked into a house already full of forced prayer and old terror and made it theirs. To them that is good ground, broken in and waiting.
